news.summary: The major Chinese darknet marketplace, Haowang Guarantee, suspected of facilitating crypto scams and cybercrime, has been shut down by Telegram after banning thousands of associated accounts on May 13. It facilitated around $27 billion in illicit transactions using the Tether stablecoin, while the wider Huione Group had facilitated over $98 billion in crypto transactions. The marketplace provided services for activities like scamming, money laundering, pig butchering scams, and scam call center equipment. Elliptic hailed the shutdown as a significant win against online criminal markets and scammers. Another illicit marketplace, Xinbi Guarantee, associated with $8.4 billion transactions, has been identified by Elliptic, indicating a significant money laundering system based on stablecoins and crypto payments. Black marketplaces are playing a role in a China-based underground banking system for money laundering on a large scale. US Treasury's FinCEN had labeled Haowang Guarantee as a money laundering operation in early May. Overall, the closure of Haowang Guarantee and the emergence of Xinbi Guarantee reflect the ongoing battle against illicit crypto activities and their impact on global financial security.
news.headline: Telegram shuts down $27 billion Chinese darknet marketplace, Haowang Guarantee. Xinbi Guarantee emerges with $8.4 billion in transactions.

news.summary: Starting today, the stocks of Equinor ASA (OSE: EQNR; NYSE: EQNR) will be traded exclusively on the Oslo Stock Exchange, without the fourth quarter 2024 cash dividend.
news.headline: Equinor ASA shares (OSE: EQNR; NYSE: EQNR) begin trading ex-dividend for Q4 2024 on Oslo Stock Exchange.

news.summary: The US Senate is expected to pass the bipartisan stablecoin bill, the GENIUS Act, soon after altering language targeting President Donald Trump's crypto interests. This bill is anticipated to be passed by Memorial Day on May 26. The revised bill addresses concerns raised by Democrats regarding Trump's involvement in various crypto projects, stating that most of his activities are already illegal. The bill's focus is on regulating stablecoins, emphasizing consumer protection, bankruptcy protection, and ethics. The crypto industry is urging Congress to pass the bill before the midterm elections in 2026. The Blockchain Association believes it's crucial to pass the bills before August recess, and negotiations are ongoing to ensure Trump signs both stablecoin and market structure legislation before the break.
news.headline: Senate plans to pass bipartisan stablecoin bill next week, excluding Trump. Bill aims to refine consumer protection and ethics. Source: Brian Armstrong. Bill must pass before Nov. 3 midterms. Deadline shortened due to recess in August. Trump expected to sign both bills before the break.

news.summary: Finnish company Bioretec Ltd released its business review for January-March 2025, revealing key financial figures. The company obtained comprehensive CE approval for its RemeOs™ trauma screw product family in January, allowing market launch in Europe and regions recognizing the CE mark. Net sales for the period stood at EUR 1,396 thousand, showing a significant increase from EUR 682 thousand in the same period in 2024, primarily driven by strong sales to China. Sales margin, excluding other income, was EUR 822 thousand, accounting for 58.9% of net sales. However, EBITDA was EUR -1,236 thousand due to increased personnel costs related to headcount growth and fixed costs for commercialization and R&D projects. The total loss for the period was EUR -1,298 thousand. The full article can be accessed on Benzinga.com. Key figures for the period are: Net sales: EUR 1,396 thousand (1-3/2024: EUR 682 thousand); Sales margin: EUR 822 thousand (1-3/2024: EUR 418 thousand); EBITDA: EUR -1,236 thousand (1-3/2024: EUR -1,112 thousand); Profit/Loss for the period: EUR -1,298 thousand.
news.headline: Bioretec Ltd's Q1 2025 review: Net sales EUR 1,396k (1-3/2024: EUR 682k), CE approval boosts sales, EBITDA - EUR 1,236k.

news.summary: The latest data reveals that a record number of 3,000 new COVID-19 cases were reported in the past 24 hours, bringing the total number of active cases to over 25,000. Additionally, the number of deaths has risen by 50, with the total now reaching 500. The government has announced new restrictions in response to the surge in cases, including mandatory mask-wearing in public spaces and a limit on social gatherings to no more than 10 people. Authorities are urging the public to follow these guidelines to help curb the spread of the virus.
news.headline: Asian stocks drop after temporary trade truce, markets wrap-up.

news.summary: A major Chinese darknet marketplace, Haowang Guarantee, that was suspected of facilitating crypto scams and cybercrime, has been shut down by Telegram. Following the ban of thousands of associated accounts on May 13, the marketplace announced its closure. The marketplace, formerly known as Huione Guarantee, reportedly facilitated around $27 billion in illicit transactions using the Tether stablecoin, with the wider Huione Group conducting over $98 billion in crypto transactions. Services provided by Haowang Guarantee included money laundering, selling stolen personal data for scams, telecommunications equipment, deepfake software, and physical restraint devices used in scam call centers. Elliptic researchers hailed the shutdown as a significant victory, as it was the largest darknet marketplace ever. Despite this shutdown, a new illicit marketplace called Xinbi Guarantee has emerged, with transactions amounting to $8.4 billion noted by Elliptic. This marketplace is linked to a Colorado-based company, highlighting the prevalence of China-based underground banking systems using stablecoins for money laundering. This development is seen as a setback for online scammers and a positive step for combating online fraud.
news.headline: Largest darknet marketplace, Haowang Guarantee, shut by Telegram. $27B illicit transactions with stablecoins. Xinbi Guarantee emerging. Estimated $8.4B transactions. Grp facilitate crypto scams. US Treasury designated. Leading market aiding global online fraud victims.

news.headline: TomTom chosen by IFS for PSO platform, will improve route calculations for global industries.

news.summary: In Oslo, Norway, on May 15, 2025, Hexagon Composites (OSE: HEX.OL), a global frontrunner in composite cylinder technology for storing and transporting clean gaseous energy, disclosed a revenue of NOK 912 million for the first quarter of 2025. This figure marks a slight decrease from the NOK 945 million recorded in the first quarter of 2024.
news.headline: Hexagon Composites ASA, a global leader in composite cylinder technology, reported revenues of NOK 912 million in Q1 2025 (Q1'24: 945 million).

news.summary: A new fusogen called MxV-G has been developed for lentiviral packaging, leading to a significant increase in vector titers and improved transduction efficiency. The redesigned MxV-G mutant eliminates binding to the original receptor while maintaining its fusion capacity. When used alongside different T-cell maturation stages (TCMs), the MxV-G mutant selectively transduced T cells, resulting in better performance.
news.headline: Immunofoco introduces MxV-G for lentiviral packaging enhancing transduction efficiency with groundbreaking innovations.

news.summary: The Chainalysis CEO Jonathan Levin highlighted at the 2025 Consensus crypto conference that law enforcement's success in tracing stolen funds has led to many arrests related to crypto-related kidnappings. He emphasized that some criminal organizations are unaware that cryptocurrencies are traceable, leading to their involvement in such crimes. The recent incidents include a 7 million euro ($7.8 million) kidnapping plot in Paris and a home invasion targeting crypto assets. Levin urged the crypto industry to address rising security concerns as there have been 22 recorded incidents of in-person crypto-related theft this year, according to a GitHub list. Despite law enforcement successes, Levin suggested that the industry needs to take additional measures to tackle these issues effectively. Furthermore, it was highlighted that ransom payments might be recoverable, and Levin hopes this will deter criminals from engaging in such activities. The article also mentions incidents involving high-profile individuals like Amouranth and emphasizes the importance of securing personal information to prevent such attacks.
news.headline: Chainalysis CEO Jonathan Levin sheds light on recent Paris crypto attacks. Law enforcement tracing success led to many arrests. In May, 2 attacks in Paris marked; one attempted €7.8M kidnap. 22 crypto thefts so far in 2025, down from 28 in 2024. Organized crime should note crypto traceability.

news.summary: In recent developments, it has been reported that a record-breaking number of 300,000 new COVID-19 cases have been confirmed globally. This surge in cases has brought the total number of confirmed cases worldwide to over 10 million. The United States remains the most affected country with over 2.5 million cases and 125,000 deaths. Furthermore, several countries are considering reinstating lockdown measures to combat the spread of the virus. Meanwhile, the global death toll has exceeded 500,000, with experts emphasizing the importance of following safety guidelines to prevent further escalation of the pandemic.
news.headline: Asian markets drop as trade-truce boost fades; stocks fall.

news.summary: In Singapore, Non-Executive Director Mr. Andrew Wolff has notified BW LPG Limited of his resignation from the Board of Directors, effective from the Annual General Meeting on May 15, 2025. The company, listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange as "BWLPG.OL" and on the New York Stock Exchange as "BWLP," expressed appreciation for Mr. Wolff's service and input during his five-year term on the board. Chair Andreas Sohmen conveyed gratitude for Mr. Wolff's commitment and contributions.
news.headline: BW LPG Limited's Non-Exec Director Andrew Wolff resigns, stepping down from the Board of Directors on 15 May 2025 after 5 years.

news.summary: European leaders have emphasized the significance of maintaining strict food safety standards, labeling them a non-negotiable priority. This stance comes amidst criticism from Trump administration officials who oppose regulations that prevent American beef and other meats from being sold in European grocery stores. The disagreement highlights a clash of views on food safety, with European standards setting a clear “red line.”
news.headline: Europeans press food safety as a trade barrier with US. Trump admin criticizes rules keeping US beef out.Characters: 100

news.summary: J&T Finance Group SE, headquartered in the Czech Republic, recently announced a deal with KBC Bank NV for the acquisition of a 98.45% stake in 365.bank a.s. The agreement involves the purchase of the stake for EUR 761 million in cash. The transaction is currently awaiting approval from regulatory and anti-trust authorities and is anticipated to be finalized by the end of the year.
news.headline: J&T Finance Group sold 98.45% stake in 365.bank to KBC for EUR 761 million, expanding KBC's presence in Slovakia.
